zotero.abstractNote: This paper presents a robotic system for the collaborative assembly of vertical elements in timber construction. The system uses a vision-based tactile sensor to detect the position and orientation of the elements during the assembly process. The robot is able to reposition the elements in real-time based on the tactile feedback, allowing for a more precise and efficient assembly process. The system is evaluated through a series of experiments, demonstrating its effectiveness in a man-machine collaborative environment.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 37th eCAADe and 23rd SIGraDi Conference
